data_in.description: Raw TWIM-MS arrival-time data (example data from HinesLab/MOCCal repository)
CCS calibration reference template with known calibrant compounds and their CCS values

data_out.description: Calibrated CCS values table with ion identifiers and corresponding collision cross section measurements

domain_knowledge: TWIM platforms record arrival time (time ion reaches detector) rather than drift time (time spent in TWIM cell), requiring explicit conversion for CCS calculation.
CCS calibration requires a set of reference compounds with known collision cross section values to establish the arrival-time-to-CCS mapping function.
Class-specific calibration is necessary because different biomolecular classes (proteins, lipids, metabolites) may have distinct arrival-time-to-CCS relationships.
Calibration templates must contain arrival-time measurements for the same reference compounds run under identical instrument conditions as the experimental data.

direct_checks: verify that github:HinesLab__MOCCal repository is accessible and contains calibration data templates and example data files
verify file_exists for calibration template file(s) in the MOCCal repository root or designated calibration/ subdirectory
verify file_exists for example arrival-time data file(s) in the MOCCal repository
script_runs: execute MOCCal calibration module with example arrival-time data and calibration template as inputs without errors
verify output_matches_reference: confirm that CCS values produced by the calibration transform are numeric, non-negative, and within physically plausible range for biomolecules (typically 50–500 Ų)
expert_review: assess whether the calibration transform correctly converts TWIM arrival-time values to CCS values according to the underlying ion-mobility physics (requires domain expertise in mass spectrometry and ion-mobility separation)
evaluate whether the produced CCS values are consistent with known reference compounds or literature values for the biomolecular classes being calibrated

Functionality includes collision'

cmd: import sys; from scipy.interpolate import interp1d; import json; raw_at = json.load(open('{inputs.raw_arrival_times}')); cal_ref = json.load(open('{inputs.calibration_template}')); ref_at = [c['arrival_time'] for c in cal_ref['calibrants']]; ref_ccs = [c['ccs'] for c in cal_ref['calibrants']]; calib_func = interp1d(ref_at, ref_ccs, kind='linear', fill_value='extrapolate'); ccs_values = [{'ion_id': ion['id'], 'arrival_time': ion['arrival_time'], 'ccs': float(calib_func(ion['arrival_time']))} for ion in raw_at['ions']]; json.dump({'calibrated_ccs': ccs_values}, sys.stdout, indent=2)

task_description: Apply CCS calibration transform to convert raw TWIM-MS arrival-time values to collision cross section (CCS) values using MOCCal calibration templates and example data from the HinesLab/MOCCal repository.
task_objective: Apply the MOCCal calibration transform to convert raw TWIM-MS arrival-time values into collision cross section (CCS) values.
title: Implement CCS Calibration module for TWIM-MS arrival-time data

workflow_description: 1. Load raw arrival-time data and calibration template from MOCCal repository example files. 2. Parse calibration reference compounds and their known CCS values from the template. 3. Establish arrival-time-to-CCS mapping function using calibration reference points. 4. Apply calibration transform to experimental arrival-time values to produce calibrated CCS values. 5. Output calibrated CCS table with corresponding ion identifiers.
